chapter: Chapter: Electricity – Magnetic and Heating Effects

questions.question: Which condition will make a bulb glow brightest in a circuit?
questions.explanation: A closed circuit with good conductors allows maximum current flow and the bulb glows brightly.

questions.question: If the number of turns in a coil of an electromagnet is doubled, what happens to its magnetic strength?
questions.explanation: Increasing the number of turns in the coil strengthens the magnetic field.

questions.question: Which factor does NOT affect the strength of an electromagnet?

options.A: Number of turns in the coil

questions.explanation: The color of the wire has no effect on magnetic strength.

questions.question: What will happen if the direction of electric current in a coil is reversed?
questions.explanation: Reversing current changes the direction of the magnetic field.

questions.question: Which property of heating elements allows them to produce more heat?
questions.explanation: Heating elements are made of materials with high resistance to produce heat.

questions.question: Which situation best demonstrates the magnetic effect of current?

options.B: Compass needle deflecting near a wire

questions.explanation: A compass needle deflects due to the magnetic field around a current-carrying wire.

questions.question: Why are heating coils made of alloys instead of pure metals?

options.B: They have high resistance and high melting point

questions.explanation: Alloys have high resistance and high melting points, making them suitable for heating elements.

questions.question: Which of the following best explains the working of an electric bell?
questions.explanation: Electric bells operate using electromagnets created by current.

questions.question: What happens when too many appliances are connected to a single circuit?
questions.explanation: Too many appliances cause excessive current, leading to overloading.

questions.question: Which phenomenon occurs when live and neutral wires touch directly?
questions.explanation: Direct contact of wires causes a short circuit.

questions.question: Why is soft iron used as the core of electromagnets?

options.B: It loses magnetism quickly when current stops

questions.explanation: Soft iron becomes magnetized easily and loses magnetism quickly.

questions.question: Which energy transformation occurs in an electric bell?
questions.explanation: Electric bells convert electrical energy into magnetic energy and then sound.

questions.question: What happens to the heating effect if current increases?
questions.explanation: Higher current produces more heat in a conductor.

questions.question: Which device protects electrical circuits from excessive current?
questions.explanation: A fuse melts and breaks the circuit when current becomes too high.

questions.question: Which factor mainly determines the heating effect in a wire?
questions.explanation: Heat produced depends mainly on resistance of the conductor.

questions.question: What will happen if insulation around a wire is damaged?
questions.explanation: Damaged insulation exposes wires and can cause electric shock.

questions.question: Which device converts electrical energy into both heat and light?
questions.explanation: Bulbs convert electrical energy into light and some heat.

questions.question: Why are electromagnets used in scrap yard cranes?

options.B: They can lift heavy iron objects and release them easily

questions.explanation: Electromagnets can be switched on to lift metal and off to release it.

questions.question: Which phenomenon demonstrates the link between electricity and magnetism?
questions.explanation: The deflection of a compass shows magnetic field due to electric current.

questions.question: What happens to an electromagnet when electric current stops?
questions.explanation: Electromagnets work only when current flows.

questions.question: Which component in a circuit mainly controls current flow?
questions.explanation: Switches control whether current flows or stops.

questions.question: Which energy conversion occurs in an electric iron?
questions.explanation: Electric irons convert electrical energy into heat.

questions.question: Which factor will reduce heating in a wire?
questions.explanation: Lower current results in less heat generation.

questions.question: Which scientific discovery first demonstrated the magnetic effect of current?
questions.explanation: The compass deflection experiment showed magnetic fields around current-carrying wires.

questions.question: Which device in a circuit converts electrical energy mainly into sound energy?
questions.explanation: Electric bells convert electrical energy into sound using electromagnets.
